note: Significant flare to the south of Active Region 3842. There is also a simultaneous less significant occulted flare from behind the northeast limb.
Source location and active region number based on SDO AIA 131 imagery
The main source of brightening is AR3841 (N13W09), however there is some contribution from AR 3842 (S15E04) as well.
This flare was observed in GOES-S as GOES-P was in an eclipse.
There is a simultaneous flare from the SE limb (~S10E80).
Active Region and source location based ON SDO AIA 131 imagery. Start, stop and peak times based on GOES Primary X-Ray flux.

Following the X2.1-class flare from AR3842 (S16W60) peaking at 2024-10-07T19:13Z, x-ray flux subsided to M7-levels before gradually rising again in association with continued flare activity and energetic enhancement in the magnetic field structure high above Active Region 3842 as seen best in SDO 131 imagery.
Simultaneous flaring from a region near N21E62.
First of a double peaked flare from AR 3852, second peak of M1.2 at 2024-10-10T12:19Z.
Second of a double peaked flare from AR 3852, first peak of M1.1 at 2024-10-10T12:25Z. Flare end time manually calculated from timeline inspection.
This flare occurs between Active Regions 3852(S10E17) and 3854(S05E25), but seems more magnetically connected to AR3852
This flare is the second peak of a long duration flare beginning at 2024-10-11T15:48Z, first peaking at M1.4 before dipping and later rising to M2.1
Long duration solar flare which likely originates from Active Region 13842 beyond the western limb.
This flare directly follows the M1.7-class flare from AR 13848 peaking at 2024-10-15T18:18Z.

First of four back-to-back peaks from AR 3854/3852, each distinct in SDO AIA 131. This peak is clearly from AR 3854 directly preceeding an M3.7 from AR 3852 peaking at 2024-10-16T03:23Z. End time chosen manually due to the following M3.7 flare's start time cutting off this flare's decay in the timeline.

Second of four back-to-back peaks from AR 3854/3852, each distinct in SDO AIA 131. This peak is clearly from AR 3852 directly following an M3.0 peak from AR 3842 peaking at 2024-10-16T03:19Z. End time manually calculated here via timeline inspection.

Third of four back-to-back peaks from AR 3854/3852, each distinct in SDO AIA 131. This peak follows the M3.7 from AR 3852, both observed as a separate more SW brightening and with a start time just barely after the M3.7 reached its end time. End time manually calculated here via timeline inspection.
Fourth of four back-to-back peaks from AR 3854/3852, each distinct in SDO AIA 131. This peak is clearly from AR 3854 via separate brightening in SDO AIA 131.
The flare location is to the west of Active Region 4859.
Some simultaneous brightening from AR 3856 (N10W35).
Long duration flare associated with eruptive activity on southeastern limb.
The first short minor peak of a double peaked flare, with the second peak reaching X1.8 class intensity.
The second, long duration, peak of a double peaked flare, with the immediately preceding short duration peak reaching M9.5 class intensity.
Active region assigned on 2024-10-28.
Occulted flare from the NE limb.  | Active region assigned on 2024-10-28.
Occulted flare on NE limb. | Active region assigned on 2024-10-28.
The main brightening of this flare was from AR 3869, however there was some brightening contribution from AR 3871 (S09E03)
Start time updated from 2024-10-31T02:48Z to 2024-10-31T02:16Z.
Some simultaneous brightening from AR 13877 (S15E05).
Simultaneous flaring also seen near AR 3869 (S18W82) near the west limb.
Double-peaked flare directly followed by an M5.5 flare peaking at 2024-11-04T15:33Z from the same AR. End time manually selected as start of following M5.5.
Double-peaked flare directly preceded by an M3.7 flare peaking at 2024-11-04T15:33Z from the same AR. End time manually calculated.
Double peaked flare with the M1.2 flare peaking at 2024-11-05T13:39Z from AR 3883.
Simultaneous flaring from an unnumbered region on the east limb, around latitude S10.

This flare was observed from a region that was unnumbered at the time, but has since been numbered as Active Region 13889. The observed X-ray flux associated with this flare fluctuated above M5.0 levels twice, but the associated EUV imagery, seen in SDO AIA 131, confirms that this is one event.
Simultaneous flaring from AR 3883 as well.
Simultaneous flaring from AR 3889 on E limb and AR 3878.
Simultaneous flaring from AR 3883 and AR 3889 on the E limb.
Flare occurs centered between active regions 13889 and 13892.
AR 3889 (S10W39) was in close proximity to AR 3892 (S10W35) and maybe also have been the source of this flare.
Majority of brightening visible in SDO AIA 131 is from AR 3901; however, there is simultaneous flaring from AR 3897 (S12W55) and AR 3889 (S09W77).
Based on SDO AIA 131 imagery, there is brightening from both AR 3889 (S11W88) and AR 3901 (S10E74).
This flare was originally observed from an occulted region beyond the northeast limb which has since been numbered as Active Region 13910.
This flare was originally observed from an occulted region beyond the northeast limb which has since been numbered as Active Region 13910.
Simultaneous flaring is also observed from Active Region 13095.
This flare is between AR 13906 and AR 13905.
This flare was originally observed from an occulted region beyond the northeast limb which has since been numbered as Active Region 13910.

No numbered active region at the location of this flare as seen in GOES SUVI 131. Some simultaneous brightening from AR 13807 (S22W30). There is no simultaneous brightening seen at AR 13901 (S10W50). Brightening observed in GOES SUVI 131; outage in SDO affecting timestamps starting at about 2024-11-26T23:52Z.
This flare was observed from an Active Region that was unnumbered at the time of the event but has since been numbered as Active Region 13917.
Sympathetic flaring was also observed from an unnumbered Active Region northeast of Active Region 13916.
This flare was observed from an Active Region that was unnumbered at the time of the event but has since been numbered as Active Region 13917.

GOES X-ray flux has three "bumps" in the M-class flaring range included in this time-frame. After close inspection of the GOES SUVI 131 imagery, it appears this flaring is all from the same active region in close succession. The initial peak of M1.2 occurs at 2024-12-06T09:20Z with an additional increase in X-ray flux to M1.4 after a small deviation in flux.
Overlapping with the simultaneous flare from behind the NW limb.
Accompanied by sympathetic flaring from the west limb.
This occulted flare was observed near or slightly beyond the west limb and is likely associated with former Active Region 13912 which rotated off the Earth-facing disk on 2024-12-11.

This flare is double peaked, and directly precedes a C2.2 flare with start time: 2024-12-13T02:24Z, peak time: 2024-12-13T02:39Z, end time: 2024-12-13T02:43Z. This initial C2.1 flare is believed to be linked to the associated CME.
This AR decayed into a plage by the time of the flaring activity.
